sections.label: Section 1526
sections.original: ἀλλὰ μεταβουλευσόμεσθα .
sections.translation: Now it ’ s time to change my plans .

sections.label: Section 1526b
sections.original: τοῦτο δʼ οὐ καλῶς λέγεις .
sections.translation: You didn ’ t say that well !

sections.label: Section 1527
sections.original: μῶρος , εἰ δοκεῖς με τλῆναι σὴν καθαιμάξαι δέρην ·
sections.translation: You fool ! Do you think I could endure to make your throat bloody ? You weren ’ t born a woman , nor do you belong among men . The reason I left the palace was to stop your shouting ;

sections.label: Section 1530
sections.original: ὀξὺ γὰρ βοῆς ἀκοῦσαν Ἄργος ἐξεγείρεται .
sections.translation: for Argos is quickly roused , once it hears a cry to the rescue . As for Menelaus , I am not afraid of measuring swords with him ; let him come , proud of the golden ringlets on his shoulders ; for if , to avenge the slaying of Helen , he gathers the Argives and leads them against the palace , refusing to attempt the rescue of me ,

sections.label: Section 1535
sections.original: σύγγονόν τʼ ἐμὴν Πυλάδην τε τὸν τάδε ξυνδρῶντά μοι ,
sections.translation: my sister , and Pylades , my fellow conspirator , he will have two corpses to behold , his daughter ’ s as well as his wife ’ s . Exeunt Orestes and The Phrygian Slave .

sections.label: Section 1.1537
sections.original: — ἰὼ ἰὼ τύχα ,
sections.translation: Ah , fortune ! Again and yet again the house comes to a fearful contest , for the race of Atreus .

sections.label: Section 1.1539
sections.original: — τί δρῶμεν ; ἀγγέλλωμεν ἐς πόλιν τάδε ;
sections.translation: What are we to do ? Carry tidings to the town ?

sections.label: Section 1.1540
sections.original: ἢ σῖγʼ ἔχωμεν ; ἀσφαλέστερον , φίλαι .
sections.translation: Or hold our peace ? It is safer , friends .

sections.label: Section 1.1541
sections.original: — ἴδε πρὸ δωμάτων ἴδε προκηρύσσει
sections.translation: Look , look at that sudden rush of smoke to the sky in front of the palace , telling its tale !

sections.label: Section 1.1543
sections.original: — ἅπτουσι πεύκας , ὡς πυρώσοντες δόμους
sections.translation: They are kindling torches to fire the halls of Tantalus ; nor do they hold back from murder .

sections.label: Section 1.1545
sections.original: — τέλος ἔχει δαίμων βροτοῖς ,
sections.translation: A god determines the end where he wishes , for mortals .

sections.label: Section 1.1546
sections.original: — μεγάλα δέ τις ἁ δύναμις διʼ ἀλαστόρων
sections.translation: Great is the power ; by avenging fiends , this house has fallen , fallen , through blood , by reason of the hurling Myrtilus from the chariot .

sections.label: Section 1549
sections.original: ἀλλὰ μὴν καὶ τόνδε λεύσσω Μενέλεων δόμων πέλας
sections.translation: But look ! I see Menelaus approaching the palace

sections.label: Section 1550
sections.original: ὀξύπουν , ᾐσθημένον που τὴν τύχην ἣ νῦν πάρα .
sections.translation: in haste ; no doubt he has heard what is happening here . Descendants of Atreus within , make haste and secure the doors with bars . A man in luck is a dangerous adversary for luckless wretches like you , Orestes .

sections.label: Section 1554
sections.original: ἥκω κλύων τὰ δεινὰ καὶ δραστήρια
sections.translation: I have come at the report of strange and violent deeds perpetrated

sections.label: Section 1555
sections.original: δισσοῖν λεόντοιν · οὐ γὰρ ἄνδρʼ αὐτὼ καλῶ .
sections.translation: by a pair of lions , men I do not call them . What I heard was that my wife was not dead , but had vanished out of sight , an idle rumor which someone fooled by his own fear brought me . But that is a plot

sections.label: Section 1560
sections.original: τεχνάσματʼ ἐστὶ ταῦτα καὶ πολὺς γέλως .
sections.translation: of the matricide ’ s — ridiculous !

sections.label: Section 1561
sections.original: ἀνοιγέτω τις δῶμα · προσπόλοις λέγω
sections.translation: Open the doors ! I tell my servants to force the gates , so that I may rescue my child at any rate from the hands of those blood-stained men and recover my poor wretched wife ,

sections.label: Section 1565
sections.original: λάβωμεν , ᾗ δεῖ ξυνθανεῖν ἐμῇ χερὶ
sections.translation: while the ones who destroyed her must die at my hands .

sections.label: Section 1567
sections.original: οὗτος σύ , κλῄθρων τῶνδε μὴ ψαύσῃς χερί ·
sections.translation: You there ! Keep your hands off those bolts ; I mean you , Menelaus , towering in your audacity ! Or I will tear off the ancient parapet , the work of masons ,

sections.label: Section 1570
sections.original: ῥήξας παλαιὰ γεῖσα , τεκτόνων πόνον .
sections.translation: and shatter your skull with this coping-stone . The doors are bolted and barred , which will prevent your eagerness to bring aid and keep you from entering .

sections.label: Section 1573
sections.original: ἔα , τί χρῆμα ; λαμπάδων ὁρῶ σέλας ,
sections.translation: Oh ! What is this ? I see a blaze of torches and men standing at bay on the top of the house ,

sections.label: Section 1575
sections.original: ξίφος δʼ ἐμῆς θυγατρὸς ἐπίφρουρον δέρῃ .
sections.translation: with a sword guarding my daughter ’ s throat .

sections.label: Section 1576
sections.original: πότερον ἐρωτᾶν ἢ κλύειν ἐμοῦ θέλεις ;
sections.translation: Would you question me or hear me speak ?

sections.label: Section 1577
sections.original: οὐδέτερʼ · ἀνάγκη δʼ , ὡς ἔοικε , σου κλύειν .
sections.translation: Neither ; but I suppose I must hear you .

sections.label: Section 1578
sections.original: μέλλω κτενεῖν σου θυγατέρʼ , εἰ βούλῃ μαθεῖν .
sections.translation: I intend to kill your daughter , if you want to know .

sections.label: Section 1579
sections.original: Ἑλένην φονεύσας ἐπὶ φόνῳ πράσσεις φόνον ;
sections.translation: After slaying Helen , you will add murder to murder ?

sections.label: Section 1580
sections.original: εἰ γὰρ κατέσχον μὴ θεῶν κλεφθεὶς ὕπο .
sections.translation: Would I had accomplished that , instead of being duped by the gods !

sections.label: Section 1581
sections.original: ἀρνῇ κατακτὰς κἀφʼ ὕβρει λέγεις τάδε ;
sections.translation: Do you deny having slain her , and say this out of wanton insult ?

sections.label: Section 1582
sections.original: λυπράν γε τὴν ἄρνησιν · εἰ γὰρ ὤφελον —
sections.translation: Yes , I do deny it , to my sorrow . If only I had —

sections.label: Section 1583
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δρᾶσαι ; παρακαλεῖς γὰρ ἐς φόβον .
sections.translation: Done what ? You frighten me !

sections.label: Section 1584
sections.original: τὴν Ἑλλάδος μιάστορʼ εἰς Ἅιδου βαλεῖν .
sections.translation: Hurled the pollution of Hellas to Hades !

sections.label: Section 1585
sections.original: ἀπόδος δάμαρτος νέκυν , ὅπως χώσω τάφῳ .
sections.translation: Give back my wife ’ s dead body , so that I may bury her .

sections.label: Section 1586
sections.original: θεοὺς ἀπαίτει . παῖδα δὲ κτενῶ σέθεν .
sections.translation: Ask the gods for her ; but I will kill your daughter .

sections.label: Section 1587
sections.original: ὁ μητροφόντης ἐπὶ φόνῳ πράσσει φόνον ;
sections.translation: This matricide is adding murder to murder .

sections.label: Section 1588
sections.original: ὁ πατρὸς ἀμύντωρ , ὃν σὺ προύδωκας θανεῖν .
sections.translation: This champion of his father , betrayed by you to death .

sections.label: Section 1589
sections.original: οὐκ ἤρκεσέν σοι τὸ παρὸν αἷμα μητέρος ;
sections.translation: Are you not content with the present stain of your mother ’ s blood ?

sections.label: Section 1590
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν κάμοιμι τὰς κακὰς κτείνων ἀεί .
sections.translation: I would not grow tired if I had these wicked women to slay for ever .

sections.label: Section 1591
sections.original: ἦ καὶ σύ , Πυλάδη , τοῦδε κοινωνεῖς φόνου ;
sections.translation: Are you too , Pylades , a partner in this bloody work ?

sections.label: Section 1592
sections.original: φησὶν σιωπῶν · ἀρκέσω δʼ ἐγὼ λέγων .
sections.translation: His silence says he is ; let it suffice for me to say it .

sections.label: Section 1593
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔτι χαίρων , ἤν γε μὴ φύγῃς πτεροῖς .
sections.translation: You ’ ll say it to your cost , unless you fly away !

sections.label: Section 1594
sections.original: οὐ φευξόμεσθα · πυρὶ δʼ ἀνάψομεν δόμους .
sections.translation: We will not try to escape ; we will set fire to the palace .

sections.label: Section 1595
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ πατρῷον δῶμα πορθήσεις τόδε ;
sections.translation: What ! will you destroy the home of your ancestors ?

sections.label: Section 1596
sections.original: ὡς μή γʼ ἔχῃς σύ , τήνδʼ ἐπισφάξας πυρί .
sections.translation: Yes , so that you don ’ t have it , and I will offer this girl in sacrifice on the fire .

sections.label: Section 1597
sections.original: κτεῖνʼ · ὡς κτανών γε τῶνδέ μοι δώσεις δίκην .
sections.translation: Kill her ; and if you do , I will punish you for it .

sections.label: Section 1598
sections.original: ἔσται τάδε .
sections.translation: All right , then .

sections.label: Section 1598b
sections.original: ἆ ἆ , μηδαμῶς δράσῃς τάδε .
sections.translation: No , no ! Don ’ t do it !

sections.label: Section 1599
sections.original: σίγα νυν , ἀνέχου δʼ ἐνδίκως πράσσων κακῶς .
sections.translation: Silence ! Your sufferings are just ; endure them .

sections.label: Section 1600
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ δίκαιον ζῆν σε ;
sections.translation: Well , is it just that you should live ?

sections.label: Section 1600b
sections.original: καὶ κρατεῖν γε γῆς .
sections.translation: And rule a kingdom , yes .

sections.label: Section 1601
sections.original: ποίας ;
sections.translation: A kingdom , where ?

sections.label: Section 1601b
sections.original: ἐν Ἄργει τῷδε τῷ Πελασγικῷ .
sections.translation: Here in Pelasgian Argos .

sections.label: Section 1602
sections.original: εὖ γοῦν θίγοις ἂν χερνίβων —
sections.translation: You are so well qualified to handle holy water !

sections.label: Section 1602b
sections.original: τί δὴ γὰρ οὔ ;
sections.translation: And why not ?

sections.label: Section 1603
sections.original: καὶ σφάγια πρὸ δορὸς καταβάλοις .
sections.translation: And to slay victims before battle !

sections.label: Section 1603b
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἂν καλῶς ;
sections.translation: Well , are you ?

sections.label: Section 1604
sections.original: ἁγνὸς γάρ εἰμι χεῖρας .
sections.translation: Yes , my hands are clean .

sections.label: Section 1604b
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐ τὰς φρένας .
sections.translation: But not your heart .

sections.label: Section 1605
sections.original: τίς δʼ ἂν προσείποι σέ ;
sections.translation: Who would speak to you ?

sections.label: Section 1605b
sections.original: ὅστις ἐστὶ φιλοπάτωρ .
sections.translation: The one who loves his father .
